markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Represent selections/cursors for operator/model/agent actors with privacy and quiet-mode constraints. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: EditorSelectionAndCursorState leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: EditorSelectionAndCursorState is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified a8f9c43c: selection_state.test.ts 6/6 - selection + cursor state. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: H1 echo-loop caret teleport (initialContent echo -> setContent per keystroke)
evidence.fix: lastEmitted identity guard + structural doc equality in RichTextEditor reload effect; RichDocumentView keeps live content in editorContentRef and passes only loaded docs as initialContent; offline harness echo removed; readOnly changes now applied via setEditable
tests: RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: keeps the caret in place while typing mid-document through an echoing parent (RED on HEAD: caret 6->13, GREEN after)
RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: applies a genuinely external document update (reload / conflict resolution)
RichTextEditor.typing.test.tsx: does not reset the caret when a parent re-render passes a structurally identical doc

findings.title: Cursor does not survive document echo: external setContent teleports the caret to document end (proven headless)
findings.evidence: RichTextEditor.tsx:156-162 runs editor.commands.setContent on EVERY initialContent identity change; RichDocumentView.tsx:204-209 (and rich_editor_harness.tsx:100-105) echo onChange JSON straight back into initialContent, so the effect fires per keystroke. Tiptap 3.13 setContent is tr.replaceWith(0, size, doc) with no equality guard and no selection preservation - headless probe on the bundled packages: caret at pos 6 of "hello world" -> after setContent(identical JSON) caret lands at pos 12/13 (doc end). Mid-document typing teleports the caret to the end after every keystroke; appended typing (what all current tests do) masks it. Also destroys IME composition state and runs an O(doc) parse per keystroke.
findings.fix: Guard the reload effect: only setContent when the incoming doc differs from editor.getJSON() semantically AND the change is genuinely external (e.g. track a lastEmitted ref in RichTextEditor; skip when initialContent === lastEmitted). Parent should pass a stable loaded-document object, not the onChange echo. Add a mid-document typing caret test (real keyboard, jsdom or Playwright).

findings.title: O(doc) selection snapshot work on every selection update
findings.evidence: RichTextEditor.tsx:177-189 computes doc.textBetween(0, from) + TextEncoder.encode(fullPrefix) on each selectionUpdate to derive UTF-8 offsets - every caret move re-encodes the entire document prefix; on 10k-block documents this is typing/caret latency.
findings.fix: Compute offsets lazily (only when a consumer subscribes), debounce snapshot emission, or maintain incremental offsets; add a perf budget test over a synthetic 10k-block doc.

findings.title: Multi-actor presence is computed but never rendered; quiet-mode path untested at the surface
findings.evidence: selection_state.ts mergePresence and remote-actor snapshots have no consumer; only the local actor snapshot is produced (RichTextEditor.tsx:180-188). The MT scope names representing selections for multiple actors; rendering/awareness wiring is absent (matches inventory ED-SEL-001 PARTIAL).
findings.fix: When collab transport lands, render remote carets from mergePresence over CRDT awareness; until then mark the surface PARTIAL in the capability inventory (done) and keep the pure lib tested.

findings.title: UTF-8 offsets are ambiguous across atom nodes
findings.evidence: textBetween(0, from, "\n") gives atom leaf nodes (hsLink, monacoCodeBlock) zero width, so distinct ProseMirror positions map to identical UTF-8 offsets; an external consumer cannot reliably map an offset back to a document position.
findings.fix: Document the mapping contract (offsets are prose-text-relative) or emit block-id + intra-block offsets instead.

parity_gaps: ED-SEL-001 selection/cursor state: PARTIAL (local-only; no multi-actor rendering)
Selection survival across external updates: FAILING until the echo-loop fix lands
missing_tests: mid-document typing preserves caret position through the RichDocumentView wiring (currently fails by design analysis)
IME composition survives a document echo (composition events not interrupted)
selection snapshot perf budget on a 10k-block document
snapshot offsets across atom nodes (hsLink/code block) round-trip to positions

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
